title: psorothamnus thompsoniae var. whitingii
intro: Psorothamnns thompsoniae var. whitingii, also known as Whiting's globemallow, is a rare and endangered desert shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Whiting's globemallow include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and its flowers are also used as a natural dye.
